videos.transcript: In this video, we'll show you the top 10 things to do in Los Angeles. This video is based on our multiple trips to the City of Angles. Don't forget to like this video, subscribe to our channel, and enable notifications. And share your own experience or ask a question in the comments below. Sponsored by Beeyond compression packing cubes for travel. The link is in the description. And stick around until the end because we have a bonus attraction for you. Here are our top 10 picks: Number 10: Downtown Los Angeles Downtown L.A. is a business and residential district packed with interesting neighborhoods and attractions. Little Tokyo, Chinatown, or Arts District each offer a rich cultural experience, and for history buffs, the Olvera Street located close to the 19th-century Los Angeles Plaza reveals the oldest part of Los Angeles. Grand Central Market, a 30,000 square-foot arcade, offers a unique food emporium operating ever since it opened in 1917. By the way, if you love food, Greater Los Angeles offers an array of fantastic food options from all over the world, from fancy dining establishments to famous hot dogs or tacos trucks, and more. Located right outside Grand Central Market, you'll find Angels Flight. With its 298 feet inclined railway, it is supposed to be the world's shortest railway. Downtown L.A is also full of green areas, like Pershing Square with fantastic views towards the skyscrap-ers, or lush Grand Park, located right in front of the iconic Los Angeles City Hall. Walk around the historic Broadway theater district, or admire architectural marvels around Downtown, like The Walt Disney Concert Hall designed by Frank Gehry or the neighboring contemporary art museum - The Broad. Number 9: Hollywood walk of fame Hollywood Boulevard is a major street in Los Angeles, home to a number of famous attractions. Hollywood Walk of Fame features over 2,600 stars embedded in the sidewalks along Hollywood Boulevard and Vine Street. The stars are permanent public monuments to achievement in the entertainment industry, bearing names of actors, directors, musicians, producers, fictional characters, and others. Look for the famous Chinese Theatres featuring handprints, footprints, and signatures of some of Hollywood's biggest stars. Visit the Ovation Hollywood shopping center with a perfect view towards the Hollywood sign. There are many other things you can do on Hollywood Boulevard, for example, Museum of Illusions, Madame Tussauds, Museum of Death, and others. Check our immersive Los Angeles 4K walking tour to experience the real feel of L.A. The link is in the description. Number 8: Griffith Observatory This impressive observatory overlooking Los Angeles offers breathtaking views of Downtown and Hollywood Sign. It also features an extensive array of space and science-related displays, including a Tesla coil, designed by inventor Nikola Tesla in 1891. Admission has been free ever since the observatory opened its doors in 1935. The view of the city from Griffith observatory can be even more fascinating in the evening. Number 7: The Grove & The Original Farmers Market World-famous Original Farmers Market started as a dairy farm, Gilmore Oil Company, and midget car racing place. The Market now consists of over 100 gourmet groceries, trendy shops, and restaurants and has been a favorite place to shop and eat among locals and tourists since 1934. Don't skip The Grove - a shopping and dining complex that mixes retail, eateries, and entertain-ment, located on the former orchard and nursery complex. If you love shopping, check our travel guide for more suggestions. By the way, our mobile-friendly travel guide covers the top 20 things to do in Los Angeles and things to know before you visit, including maps, opening hours, links to buy tickets, itinerary suggestions, and other information. By purchasing our travel guide, you are also helping us sustain this channel, so a big thank you for that! Number 6: Hollywood Sign The Hollywood Sign is one of the world's most famous landmarks and a cultural icon overlooking Hollywood. "HOLLYWOOD" is spelled out in 45-foot-tall white capital letters and is 352 feet long. The sign was created in 1923 as an advertisement for a local real estate agency, and it initially read: HOLLYWOODLAND. Due to increasing recognition, the sign was left up in its current form instead of being taken down. If you love hiking, there are many other trails with amazing panoramic views of Los Angeles worth exploring. Number 5: Universal Studios Universal Studios Hollywood is both a film studio and a theme park. It is one of the oldest and most famous Hollywood film studios still in use. The park was initially created to offer tours of the entire Universal Studios sets. Here, you can experience rides and visit film sets of some of your favorite movies like Harry Potter, Jurassic Park, Back to the Future, and others. There are also other amusement parks in L.A. and its surroundings. Number 4: Beverly Hills Beverly Hills was originally a Spanish ranch where lima beans were grown. It was incorporated in 1914 by a group of investors who had failed to find oil but found water instead and eventually decided to develop it into a town. Today, Beverly Hills is one of the world's most famous upscale places to live and shop. Visit Rodeo Drive, a famous shopping street with expensive designer stores and small private boutiques. Rodeo Dr. became immortalized by Julia Robert's "Pretty Woman" shopping spree. The area is popular with wealthy shoppers, sunglass-wearing celebrities, and tourists hoping to see fashion icons. On Rodeo Drive, you will also find the most expensive store in the world: Bijan. Number 3: Museums Los Angeles is home to over 800 museums and art galleries, which is more museums per capita than any other city in the world. Some of the world-class museums include: Los Angeles County Museum of Art or LACMA with the famous Urban Light assemblage sculpture composed of 202 streetlamps. The Broad – a Museum of Contemporary Art, J. Paul Getty Museum or just The Getty, an art museum with amazing views of Los Angeles. Don't skip Getty Villa, displaying art from Ancient Greece, Rome, and other eras. Of course, there are many other museums. Los Angeles is also home and a creative hub to numerous artists and small galleries. This video is sponsored by Beeyond, helping you save space when you travel and organize your suit-case. A revolutionary new way to organize your luggage consists of a set of small and large packing cubes. Once you're done packing, just close both zippers, compress the air out of your packing cubes like this, and voila, your clothes are compressed, and your luggage is organized. We use Beeyond packing cubes on our travels, and they are even designed to fit your carry-on. Visit Beeyond's Amazon page to get your own compression packing cube set. The link is in the description. Number 2: Venice Beach Venice is a residential, commercial, and recreational beachfront neighborhood. It was founded in 1905 as a seaside resort town. Over the years, it transformed into a free-spirited boardwalk and residential area full of funky shops, imaginative street performers, and public art. Rent a bike or an electric scooter and ride along Venice Beach, play some sports in one of many public sports areas, like Muscle Beach outdoor gym, or simply sit in the sand, watch the waves, and enjoy the vibe. The Los Angeles area is famous for its beautiful sandy beaches and picturesque piers. From surfing, sun-bathing, to sailing or boating, chances are you will find something fun to do in Los Angeles. So, if you have time, don’t forget to explore the surroundings too. Talking about Venice Beach. Don’t skip Venice Canals nearby. The canals were created in the early 20th century to mimic the famous canals in Venice, Italy. And if you love shopping, don't skip Abbot Kinney Boulevard shops. Number 1: Santa Monica Pier Santa Monica Pier is a large double-jointed and over 100 years old pier located at the foot of Colorado Avenue in Santa Monica. Its iconic entrance made the pier popular with both residents and visitors. A sign on the pier marks the end of the legendary American highway Route 66, symbolizing that this road only ended when the sea stood in its way. Since the pier is a popular tourist spot, expect it can get very crowded. The pier also contains an entire family amusement park, the Pacific Park, featuring a solar-paneled Ferris wheel. Don't forget to visit 3rd Street Promenade, a pedestrian mall walkway, dining, and entertainment complex in downtown Santa Monica, or beautiful Tongva Park, named after the indigenous Tongva people, who have lived in the Los Angeles area, a lovely urban oasis with amazing views, especially during sun-set. And here is the bonus that we promised. If you love nature, explore Echo Park. This diverse neighborhood is home to beautiful Echo Park Lake with lush vegetation, lotus flower beds, and wild geese and swans. For the ultimate experience, rent a swan boat and pedal around this beautiful lake with fantastic views towards the park and downtown Los Angeles. And now continue to our video on what you should know before you visit Los Angeles.

forget to hit that subscribe button for more exciting travel itineraries best time to visit Los Angeles Los Angeles with its Sunny disposition is a yearr round destination however the best time to visit is from March to May or between September and November when the weather is pleasant and the tourist Crowds Are thinner during these months you can enjoy outdoor activities without the scorching summer Heat or the peak season prices City Airport tips Los Angeles International Airport LAX is your gateway to the city LAX is a bustling Hub so plan to arrive with plenty of time to navigate through the terminals the FlyAway bus service offers convenient transportation to various parts of the city for around 10 USD One Way alternatively ride sharing apps like uber and Lyft are readily available costing a approximately 20 to 40 USD to Downtown LA depending on traffic where to stay in Los Angeles for the quintessential La experience consider staying in Hollywood or Santa Monica Hollywood offers a blend of Glamour and accessibility with numerous attractions within walking distance average hotel prices range from 150 to 250 USD per night if you prefer Coastal Vibes Santa Monica is ideal with its beautiful beaches and vibrant Pier expect to pay around 200 to 300 USD per night for accommodation here how to get around Los Angeles Los Angeles is a sprawling City and getting around can be daunting renting a car is a popular choice costing about 50 USD per day plus gas and parking fees for those who prefer public transportation the LA Metro system is efficient and budget friendly a 1-day Metro pass costs 7 USD offering unlimited rides on buses and trains ride sharing services are also convenient but can add up quickly day one must sea spots in La morning Griffith Observatory situated on Hollywood Mountain the highest peak in Griffith Park the Griffith Observatory is a mustsee icon in Los Angeles you probably seen it in movies like La La Land and Rebel Without a Cause inside you can gaze at the night sky through telescopes explore 60 fascinating exhibits and even visit the Samuel asan planetarium it's open from Tuesday to Sunday but you don't need to to go inside to enjoy the stunning panoramic views of the city the surrounding Mountains and the Hollywood Sign don't forget your camera this spot is one of the best photo ops in La since the observatory is one of LA's top attractions it can get pretty crowded during the day and evenings that's why I recommend visiting first thing in the morning to beat the crowds another great thing is that there's no entrance fee however if you drive there parking costs $10 per hour Insider tip while you're at Griffith Park take some time to explore it's the largest urban Wilderness Park in the United States offering plenty of hiking trails and attractions breakfast for a delightful breakfast near Griffith Observatory head to Trails Cafe nestled right within Griffith Park This Charming spot is perfect for starting your day with a delicious meal before heading up to the observatory I highly recommend trying their avocado toast which is fresh and flavorful costing around 10 USD pair it with their popularized coffee for an addition 3usd to keep you energized for your day of exploring Insider tip the trails Cafe can get busy especially on weekends so arriving early ensures you get a seat and enjoy a peaceful start to your morning amidst the beautiful Park scenery mid morning the Hollywood Sign hike everyone knows that the Hollywood sign is the most iconic landmark in all of Los Angeles placed on the mountain in 1923 the sign stands at an impressive 44 ft 13 m M tall you can spot it from almost anywhere in Downtown LA but there's nothing quite like getting up close and personal unfortunately you can't drive up to the Hollywood Sign because the road leading up there is private and only accessible to City approved Vehicles so if you want to get closer hiking is your best bet when it comes to hiking up to the Hollywood Sign you have a few different options the mount Hollywood Trail is the easiest and starts from the Griffith Observatory this 3M 4.8 km Trail offers spectacular views along the way the brush Canyon Trail is moderately challenging and is a 6.5 M 10.4 km hike starting from Canyon Drive this Trail takes you past Adam West's back cave before reaching the Hollywood sign for the more adventurous the Kena Peak Trail is the most difficult it's a 3M 4.8 km hike that takes you up to the Wisdom Tree via Kena Park and Burbank Peak although shorter than the brush Canyon Trail it features Relentless ascents and is also home to La's resident mountain lion no matter which Trail you choose the views from above the Hollywood sign are truly spectacular if you want to see the best of Los Angeles in 2 days the Hollywood Sign hike should be at the top of your list if hiking isn't your thing head to Lake Hollywood Park instead there's a great Viewpoint here of the Hollywood sign with a lot less exertion basically none Insider tip early mornings or late afternoons are the best times to hike as the temperatures are cooler and the lighting is perfect for photos the Hollywood Walk of Fame the Hollywood Walk of Fame is a mustsee for your two days La itinerary it's the world's most famous sidewalk drawing millions of visitors each year with over 2700 stars and Counting this iconic stretch celebrates achievements in the entertainment industry from actors and directors to producers and magicians spanning 15 blocks of Hollywood Boulevard and three blocks of Vine Street the Walk of Fame could take hours to fully explore but don't don't worry you can use the Walk of Fes website find the star section to look up your favorite celebrities this way you can skip the long trik and head straight to the Stars you want to see be sure to have your camera ready because the Walk of Fame offers some fantastic photo opportunities Melrose Avenue Melrose Avenue is one of the most famous streets in Los Angeles known for its eclectic mix of shopping dining and entertainment it's also a hot spot for street art featuring iconic pieces like the pink wings and the pink wall at Paul Smith starting at Santa Monica Boulevard and ending in Silver Lake Melrose Avenue offers a wide array of stores and restaurants be prepared it's easy to spend a lot of time here because everything is so tempting whether you're after a glamorous dress vintage clothes or unique Decor you'll find plenty of options along this vibrant Street popular shopping spots include decades the real re and Fred seal lunch for a fantastic Lunch Spot close to Melrose Avenue head to GR ASAS Madre a trendy restaurant offering delicious plant-based Mexican Cuisine located just a short walk from the main shopping area this spot is perfect for a midday break I highly recommend trying their famous jackf fruit tacos which are bursting with flavors and cost around 18 USD pair it with a refreshing housemade agresa for an additional 5 USD Insider tip the outdoor patio is a great place to sit and people watch while enjoying your meal so ask for a patio table if the weather is nice afternoon Urban lights at LMA Urban light is an iconic art installation at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art lakma one of the best museums in the city this stunning exhibit features 202 restored Street lamps from the 1920s and 1930s that once lit up the streets of California known as The Forest of City Lights Urban light offers a truly unique experience as you walk through the lamps you'll notice they seem to arrange themselves at different angles depending on where you stand creating a mesmerizing and slightly disorienting effect while lakma requires an entrance fee visiting Urban light is completely free and you can check it out any time of day however it's especially magical at night when the lamps illuminate the area making it one of the most instagrammable spots in La evening Beverly Hills and Rodeo Drive if you're spending 2 Days in Los Angeles then visiting Beverly Hills and heading down Road Deo Drive should be at the top of your list Rodeo Drive is a famous 2m Street of Beverly Hills known for its celebrity sightings it's one of the best places in LA to spot Hollywood stars going about their everyday lives this iconic street is home to over 100 luxury boutiques and luxury jewelry stores including Gucci Cartier and Dior if you're looking to experience the High Life Beyond celebrity spotting Rodeo Drive is your place to be you'll find plenty of five-star spas and beauty salons but be prepared to spend some ser ious cash if you decide to treat yourself even if you're just happy to wander and take in the sights or if you're a pretty woman fan strolling down this beautiful Palm lined street is a delightful experience another must visit spot in Beverly Hills is the iconic Beverly Hills Hotel it's one of the best places to stay in Los Angeles but even if you're lodging elsewhere admiring its stunning exterior is worth the trip Insider tip for the best chance of spotting a celebrity visit Rodeo Drive on weekday after afternoons when the shops are less crowded and the stars are more likely to be out and about dinner for a delightful dinner near Beverly Hills and Rodeo Drive head to spago Beverly Hills an iconic restaurant by celebrity chef Wolf Gang Puck located just a short walk from Rodeo Drive spago offers a fantastic dining experience I recommend trying their signature smoked salmon Pizza which is a unique and delicious dish costing around 34 USD for dessert don't miss the de de and chocolate sule which is an additional 16 USD Insider tip make sure to book a reservation in advance as spago is a popular spot and fills up quickly especially in the evenings the patio seating offers a lovely Ambiance perfect for a memorable dinner day two explore LA's Coastal Treasures morning Venice Beach Venice Beach is often one of the first things that comes to mind when someone mentions Los Angeles and for good reason it's the city's most famous Beach and it has so much to offer located south of Santa Monica along the Pacific Coast Highway Venice Beach is a must visit when spending 2 Days in La it's the perfect place to kick back and relax after all your exploring with great dining options quirky stores and plenty of sightseeing be sure to check out the skate plaza and Muscle Beach two of the most popular attractions take a stroll along the 2.5m Palm lined Boardwalk for stunning views of the beach and ocean you might even catch a basketball game or two for a more energetic exploration rent a bicycle for about 20 USD and Ride Along venice's stretch of the Marvin broad Coastal Trail breakfast for a fantastic breakfast near Venice Beach head to jua a trendy spot known for its delicious and fresh offerings located just a short walk from the beach it's the perfect place to start your day I highly recommend trying their smoked fish plate which comes with a variety of Smoked Fish Fresh bagels and all the fixing for about 18 USD pair it with a freshly squeezed orange juice for an additional 6 USD Insider tip jua can get busy especially on weekends so arriving early not only gets you the best selection but also a prime spot in their Charming outdoor seating area midm morning the Getty the Getty is a stunning 19th 20th and 21st century photographs although there is a small fee to view the photograph exhibits if you're not well versed in art consider taking a guided tour to enrich your experience options include tours of the Cent's impressive architecture and Gardens a collection highlights tour and in-depth tours focused on specific areas of artwork but it's not just the exhibits that draw people to the Getty the building itself is an architectural Masterpiece designed by the renowned architect Richard Meyer on site you'll find a restaurant two cafes gift shops and an extensive 86 acre garden featuring over 500 species of plants the Getty is open Tuesday to Sunday from 10:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. and admission is free except for the photography exhibit Malibu if you've only got 2 Days with spectacular views carnival games and street performers the Santa Monica Pier is one of California's top attractions the highlight of the pier is the variety of Fairground rides especially the famous Pacific wheel it's the only solar powerered ferris wheel in the world offering panoramic views of the coastline for about 10 USD per ride while it's Scenic during the day the pier is especially magical at night when all the rides and the ferris wheel light up dinner for a fantastic dinner near Santa Monica Pier head to the Albright a popular seafood restaurant right on the pier I highly From the world-famous  Hollywood Sign and Walk of Fame, to the glitz and glamour of Rodeo Drive in Beverly Hills, Los Angeles has some amazing attractions. The famous Venice Beach boardwalk is a must-see attraction, with street performers, vendors, and outdoor fitness equipment.  Los Angeles is also home to a number of world-renowned museums and art institutions, such as the Getty Center, the Museum of Contemporary Art, and the California Science Center. Visit Griffith Observatory, to see panoramic views of the city and the stars at night, or the Griffith Park, one of the largest urban parks in North America, where you can hike, have a picnic or visit the Griffith Park Zoo.

videos.description:
Los Angeles, the sprawling metropolis on the west coast of the United States, is a city that captivates visitors with its diverse attractions, vibrant culture, and endless entertainment options.

As the heart of the entertainment industry, Los Angeles California offers a unique blend of glamour, natural beauty, and urban excitement that draws millions of tourists each year.

One of the best things to do in Los Angeles is to explore its iconic neighborhoods, each with its own distinct character. From the star-studded streets of Hollywood to the bohemian vibes of Venice Beach, there's always something new to discover. The city's famous beaches, including Santa Monica and Malibu, provide the perfect backdrop for relaxation and outdoor activities, making them top things to do in Los Angeles for both locals and tourists alike.

For those seeking fun things to do in Los Angeles, the city's world-class theme parks are a must-visit. Universal Studios Hollywood offers thrilling rides and behind-the-scenes glimpses of movie magic, while nearby Disneyland Resort in Anaheim provides a magical experience for visitors of all ages. Art enthusiasts will find plenty to admire at the Getty Center and the Los Angeles County Museum of Art (LACMA), showcasing impressive collections that span centuries and continents.

When the sun sets, the city comes alive with things to do in Los Angeles at night. From rooftop bars with panoramic views to live music venues and comedy clubs, the nightlife scene caters to all tastes. For a more laid-back evening, strolling along the Santa Monica Pier or enjoying a outdoor movie screening at Hollywood Forever Cemetery are popular choices.

Los Angeles travel wouldn't be complete without indulging in the city's diverse culinary scene. From food trucks serving gourmet tacos to Michelin-starred restaurants, the city is a food lover's paradise. The bustling Grand Central Market downtown offers a taste of LA's melting pot of cultures, making it one of the cool things to do in Los Angeles for foodies.

For those looking for unique things to do in Los Angeles, consider taking a Los Angeles tour of movie locations, exploring the hidden staircases scattered throughout the city, or visiting the eclectic Museum of Jurassic Technology. Nature lovers can escape the urban sprawl by hiking in Griffith Park or exploring the tide pools at Leo Carrillo State Beach, both offering a refreshing contrast to the city's bustling streets.

A Los Angeles travel guide would be remiss not to mention the city's numerous cultural institutions. The Walt Disney Concert Hall, home to the Los Angeles Philharmonic, is an architectural marvel and a testament to the city's commitment to the arts. The nearby Broad museum showcases contemporary art in a stunning setting, while the California Science Center offers interactive exhibits and houses the Space Shuttle Endeavour.

Whether you're drawn by the glitz and glamour of Hollywood, the sun-soaked beaches, or the diverse cultural offerings, Los Angeles has something for everyone. With its year-round pleasant weather and an endless array of Los Angeles attractions, activities, and places to visit, it's no wonder that Los Angeles continues to be a top destination for travelers from around the world. So pack your bags and prepare to be dazzled by all that Los Angeles has to offer – your unforgettable California adventure awaits!

videos.title: The Most Dangerous Neighborhoods in Los Angeles, California: Enter if You Dare
videos.description:
Welcome to our channel! In this video, we're diving deep into the most dangerous neighborhoods in Los Angeles, California. Brace yourself as we explore these areas that are notorious for their high crime rates and challenging living conditions. From gang activity to drug-related incidents, we'll shed light on the harsh realities faced by residents in these neighborhoods.

Los Angeles is known for its glitz and glamour, but beneath the surface lies a different story. Join us as we uncover the truth about some of the worst cities in California and delve into what makes them so dangerous. We'll discuss crime statistics, local challenges, and factors contributing to their reputation as some of the most perilous neighborhoods in America.

If you're ready to embark on this eye-opening journey with us, hit that play button now! Remember to like this video and subscribe to our channel for more captivating content on various topics related to travel, lifestyle, and exploration.

#DangerousNeighborhoods #LosAngelesCrime
tags: most dangerous neighborhoods in los angeles
videos.transcript: Los Angeles - city of angels, palm trees, and some of the most dangerous freaking neighborhoods you'll ever step foot in. I'm talking gangland war zones where you need a flak jacket just to get a taco. But I love tacos, so I devised a clever disguise - thick black glasses, pocket protector, and classic "I Heart Math" t-shirt. I waltzed right into the mean streets of Compton and strolled out unscathed with the best street tacos this side of Tijuana. But Compton's got nothing on some LA hoods. I crunched the numbers on violent crimes, and spoiler alert - things get pretty gnarly. So join me on this whirlwind tour of the most dangerous neighborhoods in Los Angeles. Just make sure to bring your bulletproof vest, because we're going in guns blazing! Understanding Crime Statistics in Los Angeles Neighborhoods Los Angeles certainly has a colorful reputation when it comes to crime. The City of Angels is home to over 4 million people from every walk of life, so it's no surprise that some neighborhoods have higher crime rates than others. The LAPD publishes crime statistics each year based on reported incidents of violent crimes like homicide, rape, robbery and assault per one hundred thousand residents in each neighborhood. The Top Most Dangerous Neighborhoods in Los Angeles Alright, so you’re thinking of visiting LA but don’t want to end up on an episode of Gangland. I feel you. As an Angeleno, I can give you the straight dope on the ‘hoods to avoid if you want to make it home with your wallet and dignity intact. Watts With a violent crime rate of over fourteen hundred per one hundred thousand residents, Watts tops the list. Once notorious for riots and gang violence, Watts remains impoverished with high unemployment. Poverty and lack of opportunity often correlate with higher crime. The iconic Watts Towers may be an architectural marvel, but the neighborhood they preside over is anything but. With a violent crime rate of over thirteen hundred percent higher than the national average, just strolling down the street here can be life-threatening. Unless you have a death wish, steer clear. Watts was ground zero for the LA Riots back in ‘92. While things have supposedly “cooled down” since then, I wouldn’t risk it. Stray too far off the main drags and you’re likely to encounter gangs, drugs, and violence. My advice? Keep on driving. Crenshaw Just west of Watts, Crenshaw has a violent crime rate of over thirteen hundred per one hundred thousand. Crenshaw is known for gangs like the Rollin' 60s Neighborhood Crips. Drive-by shootings and stabbings are common here, but Crenshaw is also home to middle-class families struggling to overcome its dangerous reputation. Crenshaw is a popular area for filming “hood” movies, probably because it looks the part. Between the dilapidated houses, abandoned lots, and ever-present threat of violence, it’s easy to see why film crews flock here. For the rest of us, it's a no-go zone. The likelihood of becoming a crime statistic is just too high. Crenshaw Boulevard has a rep for gang activity, especially at night. Not a place to stop for selfies or souvenirs. During the day, it’s not terrible but still a bit sketch. If you must drive through, lock your doors and don’t make eye contact. Mesa Heights Nestled between Downtown and Watts, Mesa Heights violent crime rate is over twelve hundred per one hundred thousand residents. Mostly residential with light industry, Mesa Heights has active gangs and drug trade. Residents point to lack of youth programs and jobs as contributors to crime. Sandwiched between the one ten freeway and dodgy Florence-Graham, Mesa Heights is an unassuming area with a shockingly high crime rate. Nearly fifteen hundred percent higher than average, to be exact. So unless you enjoy the possibility of violence with your morning coffee, head to a different part of town. Vermont Square At the intersection of the one ten and 10 freeways sits Vermont Square, a neighborhood with almost seventeen hundred percent more violent crime than the national average. Yeah, you read that right. With numbers like that, Vermont Square should probably be called “Crime Square.” Do yourself a favor and steer clear unless you want to experience LA’s dangerous side firsthand. West Adams Historic architecture and violent crime go hand in hand in West Adams. Alongside its Victorian mansions and craftsman bungalows, the neighborhood tallies a crime rate of over fifteen hundred percent higher than average. So unless you don’t mind dodging danger between oohing and ahhing over architectural details, West Adams is best avoided. Leimert Park Leimert Park likes to bill itself as the “Soul of Los Angeles,” but underneath the cultural festivals and intellectual discussions simmers a hotbed of criminal activity. With a crime rate thirteen hundred percent higher than the national average, the soul of Leimert Park seems to be danger and violence. Stick to safer areas of town unless you want to experience the city’s seedy underbelly. Leimert Park seems artsy and hip but has struggled with drugs and crime for decades. While community groups are trying to clean things up, it remains a dicey area. Unless you’re attending an event at the Vision Complex, I’d steer clear. No need to become another LA crime statistic. As a lifelong Angeleno, I’ve seen the dark side of this city. But that doesn’t mean you should avoid visiting altogether. Simply exercise caution, stay alert, and avoid lingering in the rougher parts of town, especially after dark. LA has so much to offer—just make sure to enjoy it safely. If you do venture into one of these ‘hoods, Godspeed! You’re going to need it. The LAPD has special task forces focused on these neighborhoods, but critics argue that increased police presence often exacerbates tensions without addressing root causes of crime like poverty, lack of healthcare and education. Overall, crime in LA has declined over past decades, but for some neighborhoods, danger still lurks around every corner after dark. Stay safe out there, Angelenos! What Makes These LA Neighborhoods So Dangerous? Somehow, my curiosity always gets the better of me. I mean, how dangerous could these LA neighborhoods really be? According to the stats, pretty dang dangerous. But stats don’t tell the whole story. Crime Magnet Turns out, certain areas just attract criminal activity, like moths to a flame. In places like Watts or Crenshaw, poverty and lack of opportunity have festered for decades, creating a perfect storm of desperation. Gangs thrive, drug deals go down on every corner, and violence becomes a way of life. Under-Policed and Underserved The police have all but given up on some of these spots. Response times are atrocious, and many residents feel completely unsupported. It’s kind of a “you’re on your own, pal” situation. Can’t say I blame the cops, though - these neighborhoods are rough, and they probably get sick of going in just to scrape up dead bodies or break up fights. A Downward Spiral Once an area gains a reputation for being dangerous, it’s hard to break that cycle. Businesses move out, property values plummet, and the standard of living declines. It becomes a magnet for crime and poverty, and the spiral continues. Places like West Adams or Leimert Park just never had a chance. Of course, there are good, hardworking people in all of these neighborhoods struggling to get by. But when an area slips this far, it’s tough for the good to outweigh the bad. My curiosity is satisfied - I’ll be steering clear of South Central and sticking to the safer parts of LA. The City of Angels has its dark side, and it’s not one I care to explore any further! How to Stay Safe When Visiting Dangerous Los Angeles Areas So you didn't listen and you just had to check out Skid Row for yourself. I get it, morbid curiosity is a hell of a drug. Now that you're there, how do you avoid ending up on a slab in the morgue? Some tips: Don't Look Like a Tourist Leave the fanny pack, novelty t-shirt, and "I Heart LA" hat at home. You might as well wear a flashing neon sign that says "Rob Me!". Dress like a local in casual but nondescript clothing. And for the love of all that is holy, do not stare wide-eyed at the sights around you. Act like you belong. Keep Your Valuables Hidden Don't flash cash, jewelry or electronics. Keep wallets in your front pocket and purses close to your body. Predators scope out easy targets, so don't make yourself one. Stay Alert Walk confidently and keep your head on a swivel. Be aware of your surroundings at all times. Don't get distracted looking at your phone or chatting with friends. If something feels off, it probably is. Trust your instincts and remove yourself from the situation. Don't Go Into Deserted Areas Alone There's safety in numbers. Explore sketchy neighborhoods with a group if possible. Dimly lit alleys, abandoned buildings and empty parking lots are prime spots for muggings and assaults. Avoid them if you can. Learn Basic Self Defense Take a self defense class so you know how to physically protect yourself in a dangerous encounter. Even if you never have to use it, the confidence it builds can help you avoid becoming a victim. The city of angels has a dark side, so take proper precautions. With vigilance and common sense, you'll make it out of the rough parts of LA unscathed and with stories to tell - if you dare. Stay safe! Well slap my knee and call me Sally! After that whirlwind tour of LA's meanest streets, I'm plumb tuckered out. Who knew the City of Angels had so many demonic 'hoods lurking in the shadows? From Bloods and Crips territory to triad turf wars, this place has more shady back alleys than a film noir flick. But chin up, buckaroos, there's still plenty of rhinestone cowboys and girls gone wild west of the 405. Just keep your wits about you and be sure to pack heat in your garter belt. Stay vigilant, amigos, and vaya con dios!
